name: unifyplane_website_constraints

source_of_truth.rule: All content, routes, metadata, and UI decisions must remain aligned to canonical markdown documents.

rules: Pages must not redefine canonical meaning.
Top-level pages must preserve role separation.
Claims must remain proportional to current maturity and evidence.
AI, safety, security, and governance concerns must be visible where relevant without replacing the continuity-first identity.
Components must contain only real operational parts.
Writing must remain secondary to canonical documentation.
Foundation pages must distinguish current demonstrated use, broader intended use, and future scope.
Primary navigation order must match the IA chain.
Writing must remain optional, secondary, or footer-level.
Foundations must not dominate first contact.
Components must not include disciplines, audiences, or enterprise functions.

primary_audience: assurance, audit, and control-oriented technical readers
security assurance and security architecture readers
senior engineering, platform, and technical-governance readers
secondary_audience: adjacent readers interested in safety, security, or assurance implications
rules: Primary audience signal must be optimized first.
Secondary audiences may be addressed where relevant or through Writing.
Secondary audiences must not reshape the homepage identity, top-level navigation, or first-definition language.
Homepage must summarize and route, not fully own deeper pages.
Hero must be text-first and immediately legible.
Deep pages must support structured reading, TOC, and onward navigation.
Trust must come from structure, explicit boundaries, and page-role clarity.
Do not use personalization that obscures the canonical information scent.
Navigation must collapse cleanly on mobile.
Hero must remain readable without excessive wrapping.
Cards and sections must stack vertically.
No horizontal scrolling for primary content.
TOC must degrade to an in-page jump control or compact menu on mobile.
Tap targets must remain comfortably usable.
Each route must own one primary search and reading intent.
Semantic heading hierarchy must be clear and stable.
Internal links must reinforce the governing chain.
Avoid duplication drift across Home, What, Why, Readiness, and Evidence.
Do not hide primary content behind tabs or client-only interactions unless strictly necessary.
Use static generation for canonical pages.
Default to Server Components; use Client Components only for necessary interaction islands.
Keep above-the-fold rendering primarily text-based.
Minimize client JavaScript.
Do not use heavy animation libraries or carousel patterns in critical rendering path.
Do not render markdown on the client.
Optimize fonts and images using framework-native mechanisms.
Typography must be the primary design system.
Use restrained color and quiet polish.
Prefer borders and spacing over heavy shadows.
Avoid decorative abstraction that competes with reading.
Design and writing tone must feel like one system.
Long-form narrative and foundation pages must use single-column reading layouts.
Grid layouts may be used for landing pages, summaries, and explore-next sections.
Vertical rhythm must be generous and consistent.
Maintain strong H1/H2/H3 hierarchy.
Body text must remain comfortably readable on mobile and desktop.
Avoid excessively wide paragraphs and dense bullet walls.
Use bold for high-signal emphasis and code style only for identifiers.
Boundary/status block must be reusable across readiness, evidence, and maturity notes.
Cards must support scan speed and not feel like product tiles.
Component pages must emphasize role, what is real, strengths, and boundaries.
Home must summarize and route.
Canonical narrative pages must not collapse into one another.
Foundation pages must remain deeper than the top layer.
Component pages must not read like API docs or product sales pages.

implementation_constraints.rendering_model: static-first with Server Components by default
rules: Build from content schema and route schema first, then style.
Metadata must be generated at route level.
Canonical markdown must drive both content and metadata where possible.
Client-side islands must be limited to necessary interactions such as mobile nav, TOC helpers, or theme toggles.

non_negotiables: Evidence remains visible as a first-class trust layer.
Current truth must stay separate from future direction.
Components must remain operational parts only.
Home must deliver fast signal and route deeper.
Mobile usability and low client overhead are required.
